ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and jam; 'bread food product' is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — wholemeal bread with sweet spread
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products; no exact code exists for wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and jam composite. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this composite bread-and-spread food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is two slices of wholemeal bread (approximately 80g total) spread with chocolate spread and jam, commonly consumed as a breakfast or snack in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal Bread with Chocolate and Jam — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Wholemeal bread with chocolate and jam' (record ID F05020136). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat not reported in HPB record; estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (wholemeal bread + chocolate hazelnut spread + fruit jam) and flagged accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — wholemeal/whole wheat bread with sweet spread analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 69 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for wholemeal bread (GI ~69, medium category). The addition of chocolate spread and jam does not substantially alter the GI of the bread base in a composite food context; however, the high sugar content (19g/100g) from the spreads elevates the glycemic load significantly. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (69 × 37.0g) / 100 ≈ 25.5 for an 80g serving (carbohydrate = 46.22 × 0.80 = 37.0g). Input record GI value of 69 with confidence 0.592 is consistent with this analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (19g/100g) with low potassium (63mg/100g) and moderate sodium (248mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: The high added sugar from chocolate spread and jam (19g/100g) independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; combined with low potassium (63mg/100g) insufficient to offset sodium retention, this food is not recommended for individuals with hypertension — replace spreads with unsweetened nut butter and add potassium-rich foods such as banana or berries to the meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 19g/100g exceeds the 15g/100g threshold; medium GI of 69 with high glycemic load per serving (~25.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of high free sugar (19g/100g from chocolate spread and jam) and a medium GI of 69 produces a high glycemic load per serving (~25.5), making this food unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the wholemeal base provides 4.5g/100g dietary fibre which partially attenuates glucose response but is insufficient to offset the sugar load — eliminate jam and chocolate spread and replace with unsweetened nut butter or avocado.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar (19g/100g) and moderate saturated fat (~2.8g/100g estimated) with wholegrain fibre benefit (4.5g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The wholemeal bread base provides 4.5g/100g dietary fibre which supports cardiovascular health, but the high added sugar from chocolate spread and jam (19g/100g) raises triglyceride and LDL risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this food and replace sweet spreads with heart-healthy alternatives such as avocado or unsweetened nut butter.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 19g/100g exceeds the 15g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high sugar content (19g/100g) from chocolate spread and jam drives pro-inflammatory mediators and is not suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; replace spreads with anti-inflammatory alternatives such as extra-virgin olive oil, walnut butter, or avocado.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholemeal bread with chocolate spread and jam is a common breakfast and snack item in Singapore, consisting of sliced wholemeal (whole wheat) bread spread with a chocolate-flavoured spread (such as chocolate hazelnut spread) and fruit jam. The wholemeal bread base provides a dense, slightly nutty texture and a mild wheaty flavour, while the chocolate spread adds sweetness and a creamy, rich taste, and the jam contributes additional sweetness and a fruity note. Per 100g, this food provides 283 kcal, 9.33g protein, 7.17g fat, 46.22g carbohydrate, 19.03g sugar, 4.51g dietary fibre, and 248mg sodium. The high sugar content (19g/100g) from the combined spreads is the primary nutritional concern, making this food unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis. The wholemeal base contributes meaningful dietary fibre (4.5g/100g) and iron (4.09mg/100g), which are positive nutritional attributes. The glycemic index is approximately 69 (medium category) based on the wholemeal bread base, but the glycemic load per typical 80g serving is high (~25.5) due to the carbohydrate and sugar load from the spreads. This food is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning snack and is widely available in Singapore homes, school canteens, and coffee shops (kopitiams). It is vegetarian and generally halal-suitable depending on the specific chocolate spread brand used.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record F05020136) with confidence 0.95. GI value of 69 is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for wholemeal bread. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and flagged. All other values are exact from the approved primary source.
notes: Saturated fat (2.8g/100g) is an analogous estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central composite data for wholemeal bread with chocolate hazelnut spread and fruit jam; not directly reported in the HPB Foodo record. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo record for this composite food item and are not available in a directly matching approved source entry. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (69 × 37.0g carbohydrate per 80g serving) / 100 ≈ 25.5. The input record incorrectly labelled caution items as 'Sugar-sweetened beverage' — this food is a solid bread-based item, not a beverage; this error has been corrected in the output. Cholesterol at 0.29mg/100g is a very low but non-zero value as reported by HPB.
